Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 466 upvotes, 35 comments
  2. Why Are Viral Capsids Icosahedral? 22 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. How far can you go with IX Route Servers only? 47 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 126 upvotes, 20 comments
  5. The Accidental Room (2018) 44 upvotes, 5 comments
  6. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 115 upvotes, 20 comments
  7. Reviewing Large Changes with Jujutsu 36 upvotes, 4 comments
  8. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 89 upvotes, 18 comments
  9. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance 821 upvotes, 243 comments
  10. Six ingenious ways how Canon DSLRs used to illuminate their autofocus points 67 upvotes, 15 comments
  11. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 523 upvotes, 210 comments
  12. Stop Sloppypasta 451 upvotes, 185 comments
  13. The 49MB web page 655 upvotes, 293 comments
  14. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 177 upvotes, 71 comments
  15. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 123 upvotes, 47 comments
  16. Home Assistant waters my plants 90 upvotes, 37 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 318 upvotes, 175 comments
  18. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 293 upvotes, 170 comments
  19. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 118 upvotes, 61 comments
  20. Glassworm is back: A new wave of invisible Unicode attacks hits repositories 286 upvotes, 169 comments
  21. AirPods Max 2 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. LLMs can be exhausting 260 upvotes, 172 comments
  23. Kona EV Hacking 60 upvotes, 36 comments
  24. Show HN: Hackerbrief – Top posts on Hacker News summarized daily 9 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. How I write software with LLMs 310 upvotes, 251 comments
  26. Even Faster Asin() Was Staring Right at Me 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. The emergence of print-on-demand Amazon paperback books 185 upvotes, 146 comments
  28. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Scientists discover a surprising way to quiet the anxious mind (2025) 38 upvotes, 36 comments
  30. Nango (YC W23, API Access for Agents and Apps) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 9:30pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
